title: andira humilis
intro: Andira humilis, also known as the satinwood or satin tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its seeds, which contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins. The seeds of Andira humilis have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and promote overall health and well-being. Andira humilis seeds can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and have also been used in traditional medicine as a poultice or topical application to treat skin conditions and wounds.
